requirements.summary: Implement a 2D GA Drawing Generator that renders four standardized SVG views (Anchor Bolt Plan, Cross Section, Roof Plan, Side Elevation) from the current 3D building model state and packages them into a downloadable PDF titled 'General Arrangement (GA) Drawings'.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Download GA Drawings' generates and downloads a PDF file
PDF contains all four views: Anchor Bolt Plan, Cross Section, Roof Plan, Side Elevation
Views are dimensionally accurate relative to the current building parameters
Anchor Bolt Plan shows pedestal/column positions with reaction labels
Cross Section shows clear height and width with member annotations
Roof Plan shows purlin spacing and X-bracing layout
Side Elevation shows bay spacing and wall bracing diagonals

file_operations.description: Create utility module that takes BuildingState and generates four SVG views: (1) Anchor Bolt Plan with column grid and pedestal locations, (2) Cross Section showing clear span/height with member labels, (3) Roof Plan with purlin spacing and bracing, (4) Side Elevation with bay spacing and wall bracing. Each function returns an SVG string with dimension annotations, title block, and scale notation.

Create client-side SVG-to-PDF conversion utility using jsPDF. Accepts an array of SVG strings and metadata, renders each SVG to a separate PDF page, and returns a Blob for download. Handle SVG-to-canvas conversion for jsPDF compatibility.

Create button component that triggers GA drawing generation and PDF download. On click, call gaDrawingGenerator functions with current building state, package all four SVGs using svgToPdf, and trigger browser download with filename 'General_Arrangement_Drawings.pdf'. Show loading spinner during generation and toast notification on completion or error.
Add GADrawingsExportButton to the engineering panel or export section of the ProjectViewer, passing current buildingState as prop. Place it near the existing BOQ download button for consistency.

requirements.summary: Implement LOD 350 high-fidelity connection detail hotspots on the 3D model with clickable nodes at key structural joints that open modal dialogs showing detailed connection views, and render Concrete Pedestals and Foundation Blocks beneath columns.
acceptanceCriteria: Hotspot spheres are visible at Haunch, Ridge, and Base Plate joints in the 3D scene
Clicking a hotspot opens a modal with the corresponding connection detail
Base Plate modal renders anchor bolt layout, stiffener plates, and grout gap
Haunch modal renders flange bolts and web stiffeners
Concrete Pedestal geometry is visible below each column in the main 3D view
Foundation Block geometry is visible below each pedestal, partially below ground level
Hotspot nodes are clearly distinguishable (e.g., glowing amber spheres) from structural members

file_operations.description: Create Three.js React component that places clickable glowing amber sphere markers at key connection joints (Rafter-to-Column Haunch at both rafter ends, Ridge at apex, Base Plate at column bottoms). Use raycasting to detect clicks and emit an onHotspotClick event with connection type and position. Calculate hotspot positions from building geometry and member endpoints.

Create modal dialog component that displays high-resolution connection detail views. Accept connectionType prop ('haunch', 'ridge', 'basePlate') and render appropriate detail scene. For Base Plate: show 4-bolt anchor pattern, stiffener plates, and grout gap. For Haunch: show flange plates with 2×4 bolt grid and web stiffeners. Use Three.js FiberCanvas or detailed SVG rendering within the modal.

Create Three.js React component that renders Concrete Pedestals (grey box mesh below each column base) and Foundation Blocks (larger grey box partially clipped below grade). Calculate pedestal positions from column grid layout. Foundation blocks should extend below ground plane with appropriate clipping or transparency to show steel-to-civil interface.

Add ConnectionHotspots and FoundationGeometry components to the scene composition. Wire ConnectionHotspots onHotspotClick handler to open ConnectionDetailModal with the selected connection type. Pass building geometry and member data as props.

Add state management for ConnectionDetailModal visibility and selected connection type. Pass modal state handlers to BuildingSimulator and render ConnectionDetailModal at page level with appropriate z-index.

requirements.summary: Implement the frontend Public Share view that loads projects via share token in read-only mode, and add a Share button in ProjectViewer to generate and display copyable share URLs.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to /share/:token renders the 3D building model
No editing controls are visible or accessible in the share view
A 'View Only' badge is shown in the header
The 'Share' button in ProjectViewer generates and displays a copyable share URL
Invalid tokens show an appropriate error message

file_operations.description: Add useGetProjectByShareToken(token: string) query hook that calls backend getProjectByShareToken. Return project data or null for invalid tokens. Configure query with appropriate retry and error handling.

Create public share view page component at route /share/:token. Extract token from URL params, call useGetProjectByShareToken, and render BuildingSimulator in read-only mode if project is found. Hide all editing controls (side-panel inputs, branding controls, engineering inputs, etc.). Display 'View Only' badge in header. Show error message with return-to-login button for invalid tokens.

Create Share button component that calls createSharedLink mutation with current projectId. On success, display a dialog with the generated share URL (constructed as window.location.origin + '/share/' + token) in a copyable input field with copy-to-clipboard button. Show loading state during link generation and toast notification on copy.
Add ShareButton component to the ProjectViewer toolbar or header area, passing current project.id as prop. Position it prominently for easy access by authenticated users.
Register /share/:token route in TanStack Router configuration that renders the ShareView component. Ensure route is publicly accessible (no authentication guard).

requirements.summary: Implement the 3D Annotation (Sticky Note) UI in ProjectViewer with double-click comment input, floating 3D billboard markers, and a collapsible CommentSidebar for managing annotations.
acceptanceCriteria: Double-clicking a structural element opens a comment input popover
Submitting the popover creates a sticky-note marker at the clicked position in 3D space
All comments for the project are loaded and rendered as 3D billboard markers on mount
CommentSidebar lists all annotations with element name, author, and text
Users can delete their own comments from the sidebar
Share view shows markers and sidebar without add/delete controls

file_operations.description: Add useAddComment, useGetComments, and useDeleteComment mutation/query hooks that call backend addComment, getComments, and deleteComment endpoints. Configure appropriate cache invalidation on mutations.

Create Three.js React component that renders 3D billboard sprite markers for each comment. Use yellow sticky-note icon texture with author initial overlay. Position markers at stored world-space coordinates from comment data. Make markers clickable to highlight corresponding comment in sidebar.

Create popover component anchored near the clicked 3D position (projected to screen space) with a text input field and submit/cancel buttons. On submit, call addComment mutation with projectId, elementId, position, and text. Show loading state during submission and close popover on success.

Create collapsible sidebar panel that lists all comments for the current project. Display element reference, author display name, comment text, and timestamp for each annotation. Add delete button (visible only for own comments, checked via identity comparison). Include expand/collapse toggle. In read-only mode (share view), hide delete buttons and add controls.

Add double-click raycasting handler to structural element meshes that captures elementId and 3D world position, then triggers CommentInputPopover. Add AnnotationMarkers component to scene, passing comments array from useGetComments. Pass readOnly prop to control interactivity.

Add CommentSidebar to the page layout. Fetch comments using useGetComments hook and pass to both CommentSidebar and BuildingSimulator. Manage CommentInputPopover state (visibility, position, elementId) and pass handlers to BuildingSimulator.
Include CommentSidebar and AnnotationMarkers in share view, but pass readOnly={true} prop to disable add/delete functionality. Display markers and sidebar content in view-only mode.

requirements.summary: Implement the Commercial Costing Engine with a Rate Card settings panel and real-time Total Project Cost calculation based on building quantities and user-defined rates.
acceptanceCriteria: Rate Card panel shows four editable numeric fields with appropriate labels and units
Changing any rate value immediately recalculates and updates the Total Project Cost display
Changing building dimensions (span, length, height) recalculates cost in real-time
Total Project Cost is displayed in the engineering/sidebar panel in formatted currency (e.g., $1,234,500)
Cost calculation uses quantities from calculateBuildingStats (weights and areas)

file_operations.description: Create utility function that accepts rate card values (primary steel $/MT, secondary steel $/MT, sheeting $/sqm, erection labor $/sqm) and building stats from calculateBuildingStats, then computes total project cost by multiplying each rate against corresponding quantity (primary/secondary steel weights, sheeting area, floor area). Return formatted total cost.

Create Rate Card settings panel component with four numeric input fields: Primary Steel ($/MT), Secondary Steel ($/MT), Sheeting ($/sqm), and Erection Labor ($/sqm). Use local component state to persist rate values. Include labels, units, and appropriate input validation (positive numbers). Expose rate values via onChange callback.

Add Total Project Cost display to the engineering panel. Calculate cost by calling calculateProjectCost with current rate card values and building stats. Display formatted currency value with clear label. Update reactively when building dimensions or rate card values change.
Add RateCardPanel to a tab or collapsible section in the side panel controls. Manage rate card state at page level and pass values to EngineeringPanel for cost calculation display.

requirements.summary: Implement Advanced Export options for STAAD.Pro (.std) and IFC (BIM) formats with center-line geometry encoding.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Export STAAD.Pro (.std)' downloads a valid .std text file
The .std file contains JOINT COORDINATES and MEMBER INCIDENCES sections derived from the building geometry
Clicking 'Export IFC (.ifc)' downloads a valid IFC 2x3 text file
The IFC file contains IfcColumn and IfcBeam entities with center-line coordinates
Both exports reflect the current building's dimensions and bay configuration

file_operations.description: Create utility that generates STAAD.Pro .std plain-text file format. Extract center-line node coordinates from building geometry (column bases/tops, rafter endpoints) and generate JOINT COORDINATES section with node numbers and X/Y/Z values. Generate MEMBER INCIDENCES section with member IDs and start/end node references. Return formatted text string.

Create utility that generates minimal IFC 2x3 text file. Encode IfcColumn entities for columns and IfcBeam entities for rafters with center-line geometry using IfcCartesianPoint and IfcDirection. Include required IFC header, project context, and entity relationships. Return formatted IFC text string.

Create export button group component with two buttons: 'Export STAAD.Pro (.std)' and 'Export IFC (.ifc)'. On STAAD button click, call staadExporter with current building state and trigger browser download with appropriate filename. On IFC button click, call ifcExporter and trigger download. Show loading state during generation and toast notifications on completion.
Add AdvancedExportButtons to the engineering panel or a dedicated Export section in the side panel, near the existing BOQ and GA Drawings export buttons. Pass current building state as props.
